plugin.name: xyztiles
plugin.description:
XYZ/slippy-map tile store plugin for SeisComP. Fetches map tiles from any standard XYZ tile server (OpenStreetMap, OpenTopoMap, ESRI World Imagery, CartoDB, custom servers, etc.) and caches them on disk.
Activate by adding 'xyztiles' to 'plugins' and setting: map.type = xyz map.location = https://{s}.tile.openstreetmap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png
URL template tokens: {z} zoom level {x} tile column (longitude) {y} tile row (latitude, 0 = north) {s} subdomain, rotated through map.xyz.subdomains
Example URL templates: OpenStreetMap standard: https://{s}.tile.openstreetmap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png OpenTopoMap (terrain + contours): https://{s}.tile.opentopomap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png ESRI World Imagery (satellite) — note reversed y/x order: https://server.arcgisonline.com/ArcGIS/rest/services/World_Imagery/MapServer/tile/{z}/{y}/{x} CartoDB Positron (clean light basemap): https://{s}.basemaps.cartocdn.com/light_all/{z}/{x}/{y}.png Custom tile server (with API key in URL): https://tiles.example.com/{z}/{x}/{y}.png?apikey=YOUR_KEY

group.name: xyz
group.description: Configuration for the XYZ tile store (map.type = xyz).

parameter.name: subdomains
parameter.description: Comma-separated list of subdomain characters substituted for {s} in the URL template. Requests rotate across all listed subdomains for basic load-balancing. Default: a,b,c (standard for OSM-style servers). Set to a single value or leave empty if the server has no subdomain variants.

parameter.name: maxLevel
parameter.description: Maximum zoom level to request from the tile server. Most OSM-based servers provide tiles up to level 18 or 19. Requesting beyond the server's maximum returns 404 errors.

parameter.name: tileSize
parameter.description: Edge length of square tiles in pixels. Standard tiles are 256 px. Some servers provide 512 px (HiDPI/retina) tiles, often via an @2x suffix in the URL.

parameter.name: cacheDir
parameter.description: Directory for caching downloaded tiles. Tiles are stored as cacheDir/{z}/{x}/{y} and reused until cacheDuration seconds have elapsed. Raw bytes are stored (no re-encoding), so the original format and quality are preserved. Leave empty to disable disk caching (tiles are re-fetched every session). Recommended: ~/.seiscomp/cache/xyztiles

parameter.name: cacheDuration
parameter.description: How long (in seconds) a cached tile is considered fresh before the server is queried again. 86400 = 24 hours (default — good for street/topo maps) 604800 = 7 days (good for satellite imagery) -1 = never expire (tiles are kept until manually deleted) 0 = disable caching (always fetch from server)

parameter.name: userAgent
parameter.description: HTTP User-Agent header sent with every tile request. Tile server operators use this to identify and contact heavy users. Please set this to something identifying your institution when deploying publicly, e.g.: SeisComP/MyInstitute-1.0 (contact@example.org) This is required by OSM's tile usage policy.
